title: Opsgenie Heartbeat Miss Correlated with Alert Spike
dataSources: Opsgenie heartbeat + alert feeds in one index
app: Opsgenie for Splunk (3759) with heartbeat integration
spl:
index=alerts (sourcetype="opsgenie:heartbeat" OR sourcetype="opsgenie:alert")
| eval hb_miss=if(sourcetype="opsgenie:heartbeat" AND status="expired",1,0)
| stats sum(hb_miss) as misses, sum(eval(if(sourcetype="opsgenie:alert",1,0))) as alerts by host
| where misses>0 AND alerts>5
| sort -alerts
description: Heartbeat loss plus alert storms indicate monitoring agent or network partitions.
value: Correlates synthetic monitor gaps with downstream noise.
implementation: Normalize `host` from heartbeat name; join CMDB for owner.
knownFalsePositives: Real outages, wide dependency failure, and new or noisy monitor rules; check the same service in the on-call product before you blame a single team.
grandmaExplanation: We tie a quiet or dead check to a lot of new alerts, so we know a monitoring gap may be the reason things look loud.
detailedImplementation:
Prerequisites
• Install and configure: Opsgenie for Splunk (3759) with heartbeat integration.
• Data: Opsgenie heartbeat + alert feeds in one index.
• For directory layout and inputs, see docs/implementation-guide.md.

Step 1 — Configure data collection
Normalize `host` from heartbeat name; join CMDB for owner.

Step 2 — Create the search and alert
Run the following search (then save as report or alert; tune time range and threshold):

```spl
index=alerts (sourcetype="opsgenie:heartbeat" OR sourcetype="opsgenie:alert")
| eval hb_miss=if(sourcetype="opsgenie:heartbeat" AND status="expired",1,0)
| stats sum(hb_miss) as misses, sum(eval(if(sourcetype="opsgenie:alert",1,0))) as alerts by host
| where misses>0 AND alerts>5
| sort -alerts
```

Understanding this search
Heartbeat loss plus alert storms indicate monitoring agent or network partitions.

Step 3 — Validate
Compare with the PagerDuty incident timeline and service view for the same window. Reconcile a few incident numbers, state changes, and acknowledgement times. If anything disagrees, check export lag, time zone, and which API fields we map.

Step 4 — Operationalize
Add to a dashboard, wire alert actions, and document ownership. Suggested view: Scatter (misses vs alerts), Table (host).
